'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2180005009777 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市浅井町大野郷東1042', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大野神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11439718}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ōno Shrine''' (Ōno-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11556970|WD=Q11556970|lt=Asai-chō|1=Azai}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} status was that of a village shrine. It is located south of {{ill|lt=Ōno Gokurakuji Park|Q11439660|WD=Q11439660|1=Onogokurakuji Park}}, near the {{ill|lt=Okakoi embankment|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Okakoi embankment}}. It is listed as the “Ōno Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181383 === Overview === * The date of establishment is unknown. It is said that the shrine was originally located slightly south of its present site. * It is said to have originally been a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, after Hondawake no Mikoto was enshrined, it was renamed a Hachiman Shrine; however, in {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Ōno Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now serves as the Ōno Community Center. Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Pppp]][[Category:qq]]

It is the "Ono Shrine" of the {{ill|Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}. == Overview == * The date of foundation is unknown. It is said that the original location was slightly to the south of the current site. * It is believed that this was originally a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, due to the enshrinement of {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}}, it was renamed as a Hachiman Shrine, but in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (the 3rd year of Meiji) it was renamed Ono Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now house the Ono Community Center. Within the precincts, various stone statues (including {{ill|Divine Statue|ja|神像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Statue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Idols in Shinto|WD=Q11588751}} and {{ill|Buddha in art|en|Buddha in art|WD=Q1000809|lt=Buddhist Statue|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments—presumed to have once been enshrined—are scattered about. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == (P3225) == * 2180005009777 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市浅井町大野郷東1042', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大野神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11439718}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ōno Shrine''' (Ōno-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11556970|WD=Q11556970|lt=Asai-chō|1=Azai}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} status was that of a village shrine. It is located south of {{ill|lt=Ōno Gokurakuji Park|Q11439660|WD=Q11439660|1=Onogokurakuji Park}}, near the {{ill|lt=Okakoi embankment|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Okakoi embankment}}. It is listed as the “Ōno Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181383 === Overview === * The date of establishment is unknown. It is said that the shrine was originally located slightly south of its present site. * It is said to have originally been a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, after Hondawake no Mikoto was enshrined, it was renamed a Hachiman Shrine; however, in {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Ōno Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now serves as the Ōno Community Center. Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]]

'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2180005009777 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市浅井町大野郷東1042', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大野神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11439718}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ōno Shrine''' (Ōno-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11556970|WD=Q11556970|lt=Asai-chō|1=Azai}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} status was that of a village shrine. It is located south of {{ill|lt=Ōno Gokurakuji Park|Q11439660|WD=Q11439660|1=Onogokurakuji Park}}, near the {{ill|lt=Okakoi embankment|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Okakoi embankment}}. It is listed as the “Ōno Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181383 === Overview === * The date of establishment is unknown. It is said that the shrine was originally located slightly south of its present site. * It is said to have originally been a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, after Hondawake no Mikoto was enshrined, it was renamed a Hachiman Shrine; however, in {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Ōno Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now serves as the Ōno Community Center. Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

It is located in [[Owari Province]]. {{Unreferenced|date=2014th year 10月26日 (日) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ono Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ichinomiya, Aichi|en|Ichinomiya, Aichi|WD=Q270810|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|ja|浅井町 (愛知県)|lt=Asai Town|lt_ja=浅井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|1=Azai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556970}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Status|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a Village Shrine. It is situated to the south of {{ill|Ono Gokurakuji Park|ja|大野極楽寺公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Gokurakuji Park|1=Onogokurakuji Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439660}} and beside the {{ill|Morio Embankment|ja|御囲堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morio Embankment|1=Q11488847|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488847}}. It is the "Ono Shrine" of the {{ill|Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}. == Overview == * The date of foundation is unknown. It is said that the original location was slightly to the south of the current site. * It is believed that this was originally a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, due to the enshrinement of {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}}, it was renamed as a Hachiman Shrine, but in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (the 3rd year of Meiji) it was renamed Ono Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now house the Ono Community Center. Within the precincts, various stone statues (including {{ill|Divine Statue|ja|神像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Statue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Idols in Shinto|WD=Q11588751}} and {{ill|Buddha in art|en|Buddha in art|WD=Q1000809|lt=Buddhist Statue|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments—presumed to have once been enshrined—are scattered about. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Principal Deities :* {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|Tatemizukata no Kami|ja|建水方神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemizukata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (believed to be the same as {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}) ; Co-Enshrined :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} == Transportation == * Get off at the "Ono" bus stop of the {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} and walk for 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|Meitetsu|ja|名鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu|1=Q11415661|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415661}}’s {{ill|Meitetsu (P3225) == * 2180005009777 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市浅井町大野郷東1042', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大野神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11439718}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ōno Shrine''' (Ōno-jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11556970|WD=Q11556970|lt=Asai-chō|1=Azai}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} status was that of a village shrine. It is located south of {{ill|lt=Ōno Gokurakuji Park|Q11439660|WD=Q11439660|1=Onogokurakuji Park}}, near the {{ill|lt=Okakoi embankment|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Okakoi embankment}}. It is listed as the “Ōno Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181383 === Overview === * The date of establishment is unknown. It is said that the shrine was originally located slightly south of its present site. * It is said to have originally been a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, after Hondawake no Mikoto was enshrined, it was renamed a Hachiman Shrine; however, in {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Ōno Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now serves as the Ōno Community Center. Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

It is located in [[Owari Province]]. {{Unreferenced|date=2014th year 10月26日 (日) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ono Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ichinomiya, Aichi|en|Ichinomiya, Aichi|WD=Q270810|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|ja|浅井町 (愛知県)|lt=Asai Town|lt_ja=浅井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|1=Azai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556970}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Status|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a Village Shrine. It is situated to the south of {{ill|Ono Gokurakuji Park|ja|大野極楽寺公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Gokurakuji Park|1=Onogokurakuji Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439660}} and beside the {{ill|Morio Embankment|ja|御囲堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morio Embankment|1=Q11488847|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488847}}. It is the "Ono Shrine" of the {{ill|Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}. == Overview == * The date of foundation is unknown. It is said that the original location was slightly to the south of the current site. * It is believed that this was originally a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, due to the enshrinement of {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}}, it was renamed as a Hachiman Shrine, but in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (the 3rd year of Meiji) it was renamed Ono Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now house the Ono Community Center. Within the precincts, various stone statues (including {{ill|Divine Statue|ja|神像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Statue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Idols in Shinto|WD=Q11588751}} and {{ill|Buddha in art|en|Buddha in art|WD=Q1000809|lt=Buddhist Statue|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments—presumed to have once been enshrined—are scattered about. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōno Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{infobox religious building | name = '''Ono Shrine''' (Ichinomiya)|native_name=大野神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image=[[File:Ono-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|300px]] | address = 1042, Higashi, Ono-gō, Asai-chō, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture | location = {{Coord|35|21|10.57|N|136|49|29.83|E|scale:30000_region:JP-23}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}}　{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}　{{ill|Tatemizukata no Kami|ja|建水方神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemizukata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | status = Shikinaisha・Former Village Shrine | foundation = Unknown }} {{nihongo|'''Oono Shrine'''|大野神社 (一宮市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. {{Unreferenced|date=2014th year 10月26日 (日) 11:02 (UTC)~}} '''Ono Shrine''' (大野神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ichinomiya, Aichi|en|Ichinomiya, Aichi|WD=Q270810|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|ja|浅井町 (愛知県)|lt=Asai Town|lt_ja=浅井町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai Town (Aichi Prefecture)|1=Azai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556970}}. Its former {{ill|Shrine Status|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a Village Shrine. It is situated to the south of {{ill|Ono Gokurakuji Park|ja|大野極楽寺公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Gokurakuji Park|1=Onogokurakuji Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439660}} and beside the {{ill|Morio Embankment|ja|御囲堤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morio Embankment|1=Q11488847|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488847}}. It is the "Ono Shrine" of the {{ill|Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warrant of Deities of the Engi Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}. == Overview == * The date of foundation is unknown. It is said that the original location was slightly to the south of the current site. * It is believed that this was originally a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, due to the enshrinement of {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}}, it was renamed as a Hachiman Shrine, but in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (the 3rd year of Meiji) it was renamed Ono Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now house the Ono Community Center. Within the precincts, various stone statues (including {{ill|Divine Statue|ja|神像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Statue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Idols in Shinto|WD=Q11588751}} and {{ill|Buddha in art|en|Buddha in art|WD=Q1000809|lt=Buddhist Statue|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments—presumed to have once been enshrined—are scattered about. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Principal Deities :* {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hotabebito no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|Tatemizukata no Kami|ja|建水方神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemizukata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (believed to be the same as {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}) ; Co-Enshrined :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no It is listed as the “Ōno Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}.https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181383 === Overview === * The date of establishment is unknown. It is said that the shrine was originally located slightly south of its present site. * It is said to have originally been a shrine associated with water. Before the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, after Hondawake no Mikoto was enshrined, it was renamed a Hachiman Shrine; however, in {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), it was renamed Ōno Shrine. * Part of the shrine grounds now serves as the Ōno Community Center. Within the grounds, stone statues ({{ill|lt=stone statues|Q12573007|WD=Q12573007|1=Stone sculpture}}—{{ill|lt=Shinto deity statues|Q11588751|WD=Q11588751|1=Idols in Shinto}}, {{ill|lt=Buddhist statues|Q1000809|WD=Q1000809|1=Buddha in art}}) and stone monuments, presumed to have once been worshipped, exist in a disorderly manner. === Enshrined Deities === ;Main deities :*{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Hachiman}} :*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemizuwake no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (presumed to be {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata no Kami|1=Takeminakata}}) ;Enshrined together :*{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Transportation === * Get off at the “Ōno” bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}, then walk 3 minutes. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take the bus bound for “Kawashima.” === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
